info.description: Automatically generated from API Inventory & Module Specs.

item.name: 🚀 Testing Guide & Docs
item.description:
## Welcome to tbsosick API Testing Guide

Follow these detailed steps to test APIs and Socket events:

### 1. Setup & Auth (First Step)
1.  **Login**: First, go to **Auth Module > 1.1 Login**.
2.  **Auto-Token**: Successful login korle `accessToken` automatic collection variable-e save hoye jabe.
3.  **Check**: Apni Collection-er **Variables** tab-e giye check korte paren token-ti set hoyeche kina.

### 2. Standard API Testing
- Protiti folder module onujayi organized.
- Path variables (যেমন `:id`) thakle Postman-er **Params** tab-e value boshate hobe.

### 3. Socket.IO Testing (Detailed Guide)
Postman theke Socket test korar jonno nicher visual guide-ti follow koren:

#### A. Connection Setup
- Click **New** > **Socket.IO**.
- **URL**: `http://localhost:5001` (Not `/api/v1`).
- **Auth (Handshake)**:
    - **Handshake** tab-e jan.
    - **Auth** section-e **Auth Object** select koren.
    - `token` key-te value `{{accessToken}}` boshie **Connect** koren.

#### B. How to "Listen" (Receive events from server)
Server theke real-time data receive korar jonno nicher steps follow koren:

1.  **Events Tab**: Connection tab-er pashe thaka **Events** tab-e jan.
2.  **Add Event**: `Add event` button-e click koren.
3.  **Event Name**: `Event name` field-e nicher common event-gulo add koren (ekekti event-er jonno alada row):
    - `USER_ONLINE`: Jokhon keu online ashe.
    - `MESSAGE_RECEIVED`: Jokhon keu apnake message pathay.
    - `TYPING_START`: Keu type kora shuru korle.
    - `get-notification::{{userId}}`: Personal notifications-er jonno.
4.  **Messages Pane**: Ekhon server theke oi event-ti asle niche **Messages** pane-e real-time JSON data dekhabe. 
    > **Note**: Ekhane kono JSON pathano lagbe na, shudhu event-er nam-ti add korlei Postman server theke asha data capture korbe.

#### C. How to "Emit" (Send events to server)
- Server-e data pathanor jonno **Message** tab-e jan.
- **Event Name**: Jekhare event-er nam likhte hoy (যেমন: `JOIN_CHAT`).
- **Payload**: Niche JSON format-e data-ti likhun:
    ```json
    { "chatId": "664a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8c9d0e" }
    ```
- Click **Send**.

### 🔌 Socket.IO Events Inventory & Guide

Jokhon apni Socket test korben, nicher table-ti follow koren bujhar jonno je konta apnake pathate hobe (Emit) ar konta server pathabe (Listen).

| Event Name | Action Type | Direction | Payload Example | Step-by-Step Testing |
|------------|-------------|-----------|-----------------|----------------------|
| `JOIN_CHAT` | **EMITTING** (Send) | 📥 Client -> Server | `{ "chatId": "66..." }` | **Message** tab-e event name `JOIN_CHAT` likhun ebong payload JSON format-e diye **Send** koren. |
| `LEAVE_CHAT` | **EMITTING** (Send) | 📥 Client -> Server | N/A | **Message** tab-e event name `LEAVE_CHAT` likhe JSON payload shoho **Send** koren. |
| `TYPING_START` | **EMITTING** (Send) | 📥 Client -> Server | `{ "chatId": "66..." }` | **Message** tab theke emit koren typing shuru bujhate. |
| `TYPING_STOP` | **EMITTING** (Send) | 📥 Client -> Server | N/A | **Message** tab-e event name `TYPING_STOP` likhe JSON payload shoho **Send** koren. |
| `DELIVERED_ACK` | **EMITTING** (Send) | 📥 Client -> Server | N/A | **Message** tab-e event name `DELIVERED_ACK` likhe JSON payload shoho **Send** koren. |
| `READ_ACK` | **EMITTING** (Send) | 📥 Client -> Server | N/A | **Message** tab-e event name `READ_ACK` likhe JSON payload shoho **Send** koren. |
| `disconnect` | **EMITTING** (Send) | 📥 Client -> Server | N/A | **Message** tab-e event name `disconnect` likhe JSON payload shoho **Send** koren. |
| `TYPING_START` | **LISTENING** (Receive) | 📤 Server -> Client | `{ "chatId": "66..." }` | **Events** tab-e `TYPING_START` add koren listening-er jonno. |
| `TYPING_STOP` | **LISTENING** (Receive) | 📤 Server -> Client | N/A | **Events** tab-e `TYPING_STOP` nam-er ekta event add koren jate server theke response ashole apni **Messages** pane-e dekhte paren. |


### 4. Common Testing Scenarios
- **Chatting**:
    1. Login koren.
    2. Socket connect koren.
    3. `JOIN_CHAT` emit koren chatId diye.
    4. Onno user theke message pathale apni listener events-e sheta paben.

request.description: Authenticates user and issues access/refresh tokens. Supports push notification registration.

exec: console.log("accessToken has been set in collection variables");

request.description: Registration or Forgot Password flow OTP verification.

exec: console.log("accessToken has been set in collection variables");

request.description: Triggers password reset flow by sending an OTP to the user's email.
Resets user password using a valid reset token.
Rotates refresh token and issues a new access token. Implements reuse detection.

exec: console.log("accessToken has been set in collection variables");

request.description: Logs out user by clearing cookies and removing device tokens.
Resends a new 6-digit verification code to the user's email.
Authenticates user via Google or Apple ID tokens. Handles both signup and login.
Allows authenticated users to change their password.
Used both for public mobile registration and for admin-driven account creation from the dashboard. Admin-created accounts are auto-verified and assigned role `USER`.
Supports text fields and a `profilePicture` file upload.
Marks the user's onboarding process as completed.

body.raw:
{
  "success": true,
  "statusCode": 200,
  "message": "Onboarding marked as completed",
  "data": {
    "id": "69fa332f3fc3858c40265420",
    "name": "Khaled Ahmed",
    "role": "USER",
    "email": "khaledahmednayeem2004@gmail.com",
    "country": "USA",
    "gender": "male",
    "dateOfBirth": "1995-05-15",
    "phone": "+123456789",
    "profilePicture": "https://i.ibb.co/z5YHLV9/profile.png",
    "isOnboardingCompleted": true,
    "status": "ACTIVE",
    "verified": true,
    "deviceTokens": [
      {
        "token": "fcm-token-xyz",
        "lastSeenAt": "2026-05-05T18:32:02.435Z"
      }
    ],
    "createdAt": "2026-05-05T18:13:03.393Z",
    "updatedAt": "2026-05-05T18:33:49.649Z",
    "_v": 0
  }
}

request.description: **Primary Search Endpoint**: This is the main API used to search cards.
Used to show stats on the Home screen. Returns counts for all public cards in the system and the user's own total cards.

query.description: (Optional) `public` or `private`. Filter your cards by their visibility status.

request.description: **Personal Library Endpoint**: This API is used to fetch all cards created by the authenticated user, regardless of whether they are `PUBLIC` or `PRIVATE`.
use case: User Creates a new preference card.
Fetches all card details (surgeon, supplies, sutures, workflow, etc.).
**Unified Endpoint**: Updates an existing card. This single endpoint handles both regular user updates (title, surgeon, etc.) and Admin moderation (verification).

formdata.description: Select new binary image files to append

request.description: Permanently deletes a card. Deletion is only possible for the owner or SUPER_ADMIN. This is a hard delete; restoration is not possible. Admins can also use this endpoint to manually delete cards.
Adds a card to the favorites list. Follows idempotent behavior (returns 200 OK if already favorited).
Removes from the favorites list. Follows idempotent behavior (returns 200 OK even if already unfavorited).
use case: User increments the card download count and logs the download history.
**Dual Pattern Endpoint**: Used for both single-day views (Home screen) and monthly calendar views (Calendar screen).

**Performance Optimized**: This endpoint is designed specifically for the Monthly Calendar view. It returns ONLY the unique dates that have events, making the response extremely lightweight for high-speed calendar dot rendering.
Creates a new event and automatically schedules reminders (24h and 1h before).

body.raw:
{
  "title": "Knee Replacement Surgery",
  "date": "2026-05-10",
  "time": "10:30 AM",
  "location": "City Hospital, Operating Room 2",
  "preferenceCard": "6817f8b92e4a8c1d44a91f20",
  "durationInHours": 3,
  "eventType": "SURGERY",
  "personnel": {
    "leadSurgeon": "Dr. John Doe",
    "surgicalTeamMembers": [
      "Dr. Sarah Smith",
      "Nurse Alex",
      "Technician Robert"
    ]
  },
  "keyNotes": "Patient requires special orthopedic instruments."
}

request.description: Fetches full details for a specific event.
Updates an existing event. Reminders are automatically rescheduled if time-related fields are changed.
Permanently removes an event from the system.
Single notification ke read mark kore. Tap-er shathe shathe (optimistic) call hoy background e.
User top-right "Mark all as read" button e tap korle shob notifications `isRead: true` hoy ebong red dot disappear kore.
Swipe-to-delete ba long-press menu theke notification delete korar jonno. Optimistic UI: row immediately list theke remove hoy. Failure hole row restore hoy + error toast dekhay.
Shob available legal pages (Terms, Privacy, etc.) er title ebong slug list fetch korar jonno. Admin-side e same endpoint use kora hoy CMS list view-er jonno.
Slug diye specific legal page-er full HTML/Markdown content fetch korar jonno.
Notun legal page create kore.
Existing legal page update kore.
Legal page delete kore.
Shob supplies paginated list fetch kore. `searchTerm` diye name search kora jay. Preference card create/edit screen eo ei endpoint use hoy catalog load korar jonno.
Single supply create kore. Shudhu `name` field required.
Multiple supplies ekbar e create kore. Duplicate name skip kore — response e kon gula skip hoyeche dekhay.
Supply er name update kore. Shudhu `name` field changeable.
Supply permanently delete kore. Hard delete — restore possible na.
Shob sutures paginated list fetch kore. `searchTerm` diye name search kora jay. Preference card create/edit screen eo ei endpoint use hoy catalog load korar jonno.
Single suture create kore. Shudhu `name` field required.
Multiple sutures ekbar e create kore. Duplicate name skip kore — response e kon gula skip hoyeche dekhay.
Suture er name update kore. Shudhu `name` field changeable.
Fetches a list of all active specialties available in the system.
**Admin Only**: Creates a new specialty in the system. This must be done by an Admin before users can select this specialty while creating preference cards.
Updates an existing specialty.
Deletes a specialty from the system.
Suture permanently delete kore. Hard delete — restore possible na.
Current user-er active subscription plan ebong status fetch kore. Free user-er jonno o sobshomoy ekta plan object return hoy — 404 return hoy na.
iOS StoreKit 2 theke pawa `signedTransactionInfo` verify kore. Apple server-er shathe cryptographic verification hoy ebong user-er plan update kore.
Android BillingClient theke pawa `purchaseToken` verify kore. Google Play Developer API use kore state fetch kora hoy.
User-ke manually free plan-e downgrade ba switch korte allow kore.
This endpoint is used for dashboard summary cards. It calculates monthly growth: current month vs last month.

query.description: Filter data by a specific year

request.description: Returns monthly analytics for preference cards — includes an inline YoY comparison (current year vs. last year), with `peak` and `slowest` months pre-computed server-side.

query.description: Filter data by a specific year

request.description: Returns monthly analytics for active subscriptions — includes an inline YoY comparison (current year vs. last year), with `peak` and `slowest` months pre-computed server-side.
Aggregated growth metrics for the admin dashboard.

query.description: Filter by specialty (regex match on calculated list)

request.description: it's give lists all users, with this search, filtered by various criteria, and paginated results.
Admin specific view of a user's basic info. Returns user data wrapped in a `user` object.
Update any user field, including their account status (ACTIVE, RESTRICTED, etc.).
Permanently removes a user from the database.
